_comment: The known-red proof for every gate guardrails/pre-push runs (SPEC INV-212, gate w). A gate that cannot red is a check that looks at nothing (the hollow authority-anchor gate this movement rebuilt, commit 8a0209f). check-every-gate-can-fail.py enumerates the `-- gate X:` markers pre-push invokes and requires each classified here: a `proof` names a red-first test `<file>::<function>` that drives the gate's check to a non-zero exit, tied to the gate by a `reds` token the proof file must reference (usually the check's basename); a `covered` entry names a gate that runs no independent check of its own and rides another gate's red, with the reason it holds no separate fixture. A proof is verified by structure (file exists, function defined, token present, a non-zero-exit assertion in the function body), since the suite runs it under gate b. A gate classified nowhere reds, a reasonless `covered` reds, a `cannot_red` gate reds loudly (a gate that can never fail is a finding, not a carve-out), and a key naming no local gate reds.

covered.c: Gate c invokes no check of its own: pre-push echoes a note pointing at the pytest suite. The anchor-ownership assertion tests/test_traceability.py::test_architecture_owns_every_anchor_once rides the suite (gate b), whose red-proof tests/test_guardrails.py::test_broken_suite_fails shows the suite reds on a broken assertion. This mirrors the CI carve-out ci-mirror.json already declares for gate c (folded into the test suite).
